answer:
You know, I think it's definitely worse to be used by others than to use them. First of all, when you're being used, it feels really dehumanizing. I remember this one time in high school when a so-called friend only reached out to me when they needed help with their homework. It was like, I wasn't even a person to them; I was just a means to an end. That really made me feel undervalued and disrespected.

On the flip side, using others can also be harmful, but I believe it often stems from a place of insecurity or desperation. I mean, people sometimes use others to boost their own self-esteem or to achieve something they find hard to do on their own. However, in the long run, that behavior can damage relationships and trust, which are super important, right? But even so, I feel like being used can leave deeper emotional scars because it strips away your sense of agency.

Moreover, when someone uses you, it creates a power imbalance that can lead to feelings of resentment and betrayal. I think we all value authentic connections where both parties benefit. So, in that sense, I would argue that being used is a more damaging experience. Ultimately, I find that fostering genuine relationships versus manipulative ones enriches our lives much more, and I'd choose that any day over being taken advantage of.
title: Is it worse to be used by others or to use others? Why do you feel this way?
